name: kitty
description: Linux kitty terminal tools for Codex, using one singleton kitty OS window with tab selectors for terminal listing, unified input, and screen reads.

interface.shortDescription: Send input to and inspect singleton kitty tabs
interface.longDescription: Kitty gives Codex Linux-native terminal tools for using one visible singleton kitty OS window. The model-facing interface exposes kitty_list for available tabs and windows, kitty_open for opening a new tab without sending input, kitty_send for shell commands, raw text, and key input with screen feedback, and kitty_read for the current terminal screen. Human-facing short_id values are tab selectors such as T3, while window_id precisely targets one shell window. It uses kitty's remote-control socket interface at /run/user/<uid>/codex/plugins/kitty/main.sock and does not use TCP sockets, prompt text, or window titles to infer state.
